description: The golden cousin of the classic red tumbler. This variety produces masses of bright yellow cherry tomatoes with a slightly zestier, citrusy flavour. Looks spectacular mixed with red varieties in a hanging basket.

characteristics.waterNeeds: High — daily watering essential in baskets
characteristics.soilType: Rich potting compost with water-retaining gel

care.sowIndoors: Sow seeds 0.5cm deep in small pots on a warm windowsill (18–21°C). Keep moist. Can be slower to germinate than red varieties, so start a week earlier if possible.
care.hardenOff: From mid-May, gradually acclimatise plants to outdoor conditions over 7–10 days.
care.plantOut: After the last frost (mid-May in London). Looks great planted alongside red varieties for colour contrast.
care.watering: Water daily in summer. Baskets dry out rapidly in the wind and sun.
care.feeding: Feed weekly with a high-potash tomato fertiliser once flowers appear.
care.harvest: Pick when fruits are a rich golden yellow. They have a tangier flavour than the red ones.
care.overwintering: Annual plant — compost at the end of the season.

propagation.seedDescription: Small, flat, pale yellow seeds. Indistinguishable from red tomato seeds.
propagation.cuttingsMethod: Side shoots can be rooted, but seed is the standard method.

deadheading.notes: No deadheading needed. The plant is determinate (bushy) and will stop growing at a certain size naturally.

seedSaving.method: Squeeze seeds from a ripe yellow fruit into water. Ferment for 3 days to remove the gel, rinse, and dry.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Seeds are inside the yellow fruit.

commonProblems: Drying out — hanging baskets are very prone to drought.
Sunscald — fruit can get bleached spots in intense sun if foliage is sparse.
Aphids — check the underside of leaves regularly.
funFact: Yellow tomatoes are often lower in acid than red ones, making them gentler on the stomach for some people.
